findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:305` has both finite assumptions; Mathlib provides `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq` with no finite assumptions at `mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Dimension/Finrank.lean:116`, and finite-dimensionality transport at `mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/FiniteDimensional/Defs.lean:248`.
findings.fix: State the result at the `Module.finrank` level without finiteness assumptions, or derive the target-side finiteness from `(elementaryTwoQuotientCongr e)` instead of requiring callers to provide it.
findings.issue: `twoRank_eq_of_mulEquiv` assumes both `[Module.Finite (ZMod 2) (ElementaryTwoQuotient G)]` and `[Module.Finite (ZMod 2) (ElementaryTwoQuotient H)]`, but the second is stronger than necessary under `elementaryTwoQuotientCongr e`.

findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/ClassGroup/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:170` repeats both finite assumptions and calls the generic theorem at line 175.
findings.fix: Mirror the generalized generic theorem, requiring at most the source-side finite-dimensionality needed by the chosen `twoRank` wrapper and deriving the target side from the induced linear equivalence.
findings.issue: The class-group wrapper repeats the same over-strong two-sided finiteness requirement for `twoRank_eq_of_mulEquiv`.

summary: The transport maps are at the right generic level, but the new rank invariance lemmas require finiteness on both sides even though the induced linear equivalence transports finite-dimensionality and `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq` itself has no finite hypotheses.
